read_file.description: Read the content of a file.
read_file.range: Line range to read, e.g., '1-10' to read lines 1 to 10, if not specified, read the entire file. If a single number is given (e.g., '5'), it means from that line to the end of the file.

write_file.target: Target content for replacement, only used when type is 'replace'
write_file.notInWorkspace: The file are not in the workspace, so you cannot change it

ask_user.description: Ask the user a question and get the input.
ask_user.question: The question to ask the user
ask_user.options: Optional list of options for the user to choose from
ask_user.hasCustomization: Indicates whether the user can provide a custom answer

list_files.dir: Directory path to list files from
list_files.recursive: Whether subdirectories should be included in the listing

create_sub_agent.description: Create a new sub agent. The return value is the sub agent ID

sub_agent.description: Delegate tasks to other models. Note: This is asynchronous and does not wait for the task to complete
sub_agent.task: Describe this task with natural language
sub_agent.level: Model level. The lower the level, the more complex the tasks handled

wait_sub_agent.description: Wait for the sub agent to complete the current task.

add_todo.description: Add a TODO. The return value is the TODO ID.
add_todo.task: Describe this task with natural language

update_todo.description: Update the status of a TODO.

reasoning_effort.description: Change the reasoning effort of this session

run_command.timeout: Timeout in milliseconds for the command execution (optional). If not specified, the default timeout is 5 minutes.

update_plan.target: Target content for replacement, only used when type is 'replace'

compacting_conversation.description: Compact the conversation to help you work more efficiently.

get_all_windows.description: Get all windows in the desktop environment.

get_window_tree.description: Get the component tree for a specific window. Each node exposes only its name and ID, in addition to its children.
get_window_tree.window_id: The ID of the window to get the component tree for

click_button.description: Click a button in a window.
click_button.buttonId: The ID of the button to click

input_text.description: Input text into a text field in a window.
input_text.input_id: The ID of the text field to input text into

activate_window.description: Restore and bring a window to the foreground.
activate_window.window_id: The ID of the window to activate

close_window.description: Request that a window close through its standard window control.
close_window.window_id: The ID of the window to close

click_element.description: Activate a UI element, including buttons, links, selectable items, and toggles.
click_element.element_id: The ID of the UI element to activate

set_checked.description: Set a checkbox or other toggle control to a specific checked state.
set_checked.element_id: The ID of the toggle control
set_checked.checked: Whether the control should be checked

select_element.description: Select an item in a list, tree, tab control, or other selection container.
select_element.element_id: The ID of the selectable item

scroll_element.description: Scroll a scrollable UI element by one small step. Use -1 for backward/up or left, 0 for no movement, and 1 for forward/down or right. At least one amount must be non-zero.
scroll_element.element_id: The ID of the scrollable UI element

press_key.description: Send a keyboard shortcut to the currently focused application. Supports A-Z, 0-9, ENTER, TAB, ESC, SPACE, BACKSPACE, DELETE, INSERT, HOME, END, PAGEUP, PAGEDOWN, arrow keys, F1-F12, and CTRL/ALT/SHIFT combinations.
press_key.key_combination: A key or combination such as ENTER, CTRL+L, ALT+F4, or SHIFT+TAB

reasonDescription: Reason for executing this tool (optional). The reason are as detailed as possible. If the conversation is in auto mode, it is strongly recommended to enter this parameter, as it can improve the pass rate.
hasTodoReminder:
<system-reminder>
You have unfinished TODOs, you can use the `list_todo` tool to view them
</system-reminder>
autoModeOn:
<system-reminder>
This session has now switched to automatic mode, so you shouldn't ask the user questions
</system-reminder>
autoModeOff:
<system-reminder>
This session has now switched off automatic mode, allowing you to ask users questions whenever needed
</system-reminder>
planModeOn:
<system-reminder>
This session has now switched to plan mode
</system-reminder>
planModeOff:
<system-reminder>
This session has now switched off plan mode
</system-reminder>
foreverModeOn:
<system-reminder>
This session has now switched to forever mode, you can't stop working
</system-reminder>
foreverModeOff:
<system-reminder>
This session has now switched off forever mode, you can stop working when you finish the task
</system-reminder>
foreverModeReminder:
<system-reminder>
Continue working. This session is in forever mode.
</system-reminder>
safetyEvaluatorNotAvailable: The safety evaluator is temporarily unavailable
